title: The Taming of the Asuka
wikitext:
[]: The Taming of the Asuka             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 02, 2009 5:44 pm Split from this post in the Evangelion VS RahXephon thread, for lack of RahX content and a lot of Asuka instead Xard wrote: Miss O: Asuka certainly isn't ripped off from any show. Archetype exists and I'd say it goes long way beyond history of anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Aug 02, 2009 5:47 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Xard wrote: Miss O: Asuka certainly isn't ripped off from any show. Archetype exists and I'd say it goes long way beyond history of anime.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sun Aug 02, 2009 5:54 pm Xard wrote: Miss O: Asuka certainly isn't ripped off from any show. Archetype exists and I'd say it goes long way beyond history of anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Aug 02, 2009 6:14 pm Technically speakin' Luke Skywalker has parental issues. And IIRC so did original Gundam main man (correct me if I remember this wrong). It's all about substance and how themes and concepts are handled rather than is some "idea" - like parental issues - there. I don't think Watchmen's Laurie is first comic character with mommy issues for example yet one could think her as "the" example *shrug*

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sun Aug 02, 2009 7:21 pm Xard wrote: And IIRC so did original Gundam main man (correct me if I remember this wrong).

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sun Aug 02, 2009 7:38 pm I didn't mean to say she was a rip-off.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 03, 2009 1:16 am oOoOoOo wrote: like saying NGE "steals" from Legend of the Galactic Heroes in the way it introduces Katerose , a fiery-tempered red-haired ace pilot

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:13 am Xard wrote: vl;ra;ad

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:34 am I remember reading an article where Mamoru Oshii talked about the way RahRah's creator was influenced by NGE (in Oshii's opinion, that is) without realizing it but I can't seem to find it anywhere. Would anyone happen to have a link? The debate on NGE's role in anime reminds me of the Burdach vs Burkhardt debate concerning Renaissance in relation to the Middle Ages...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:34 am honsou wrote: Xard wrote: And IIRC so did original Gundam main man (correct me if I remember this wrong).

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:36 am LiLi wrote: I remember reading an article where Mamoru Oshii talked about the way RahRah's creator was influenced by NGE (in Oshii's opinion, that is) without realizing it but I can't seem to find it anywhere. Would anyone happen to have a link?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:40 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: LiLi wrote: I remember reading an article where Mamoru Oshii talked about the way RahRah's creator was influenced by NGE (in Oshii's opinion, that is) without realizing it but I can't seem to find it anywhere. Would anyone happen to have a link?

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:47 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: It was in a booklet that came with the RahXephon movie. I don't know if it was published anywhere else but I definitely remember reading that. If I have free time this week I'll try to scan the booklet and upload it.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 6:30 am Mr. Tines wrote: where WETA got the Kate Rose name

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Aug 03, 2009 8:20 am Mr. Tines wrote: oOoOoOo wrote: like saying NGE "steals" from Legend of the Galactic Heroes in the way it introduces Katerose , a fiery-tempered red-haired ace pilot

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 03, 2009 2:01 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: Quite .

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 8:48 pm Mr. Tines wrote: When trying to figure out WTF the end of EoE meant, one of the possible interpretations I saw, one that made me feel quite sick, was remembering a horribly effective production of the Shrew.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 04, 2009 1:54 am Eoe had, out of the blue, turned Shinji into this abuser type, and had picked Asuka up just to have her stamped back down, and then handed over to Shinji's tender mercies... Sitting there slightly shell-shocked after it was finally over, the first analogy that came to mind haviing seen that listless figure on the beach was that production of the Shrew, which had been played as a dead straight tale of psychological torture. Followed swiftly by the refusal to accept anything at all like that interpretation.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 2:08 am Maybe I'm failing to see the connection. The psychological torture Asuka suffers and Shinji's responsibility for it is NOTHING like the torture Katherine suffers at the hands of Petruchio. Plus, IIRC, Petruchio is never physically abusive to Katherine, and Shinji's "abuse" to Asuka in One More Final is more a product of his own psychosis over what's happened than any genuine malice against Asuka (and, what's more, it certainly wasn't an act to "tame her"). Also, the thread split is a bit odd since you also cut off a bunch of relevant Rah/Eva talk from the other thread...

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:19 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Maybe I'm failing to see the connection.
summary:
[]: The Taming of the Asuka             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Aug 02, 2009 5:44 pm Split from this post in the Evangelion VS RahXephon thread, for lack of RahX content and a lot of Asuka instead Xard wrote: Miss O: Asuka certainly isn't ripped off from any show. Archetype exists and I'd say it goes long way beyond history of anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard »
